### Mintlify Step-by-Step Procedure Example

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/docs/ai-tools/cursor.mdx

Demonstrates structuring documentation into sequential steps, with options for including checks and warnings within each step. Useful for guiding users through complex processes.

```markdown
<Steps>
<Step title="Install dependencies">
  Run `npm install` to install required packages.
  
  <Check>
  Verify installation by running `npm list`.
  </Check>
</Step>

<Step title="Configure environment">
  Create a `.env` file with your API credentials.
  
  ```bash
  API_KEY=your_api_key_here
  ```
  
  <Warning>
  Never commit API keys to version control.
  </Warning>
</Step>
</Steps>
```

--------------------------------

### Install dependencies and start development server

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/desktop/README.md

Set up the development environment and run the dev server for the OpenCode Desktop app using bun.

```bash
bun install
bun dev
```

--------------------------------

### Install and Run Mintlify CLI

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/docs/quickstart.mdx

Installs the Mintlify CLI globally and starts the local development server. This allows you to preview your documentation site locally and see changes in real-time. Ensure you have Node.js and npm installed.

```bash
npm i -g mint
mint dev
```

--------------------------------

### Example: Start Backend and Attach TUI

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/cli.mdx

First, start the OpenCode backend server for web/mobile access. Then, in a separate terminal, attach the TUI to the running backend using its URL.

```bash
opencode web --port 4096 --hostname 0.0.0.0
```

```bash
opencode attach http://10.20.30.40:4096
```

--------------------------------

### Quickstart: Record and Replay HTTP Requests with Effect

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/http-recorder/README.md

This example demonstrates how to use `HttpRecorder.cassetteLayer` to provide a recorded HTTP client. It records if a cassette is missing, replays if it exists, and can be forced to re-record.

```ts
import { Effect } from "effect"
import { HttpClient, HttpClientRequest } from "effect/unstable/http"
import { HttpRecorder } from "@opencode-ai/http-recorder"

const program = Effect.gen(function* () {
  const http = yield* HttpClient.HttpClient
  const response = yield* http.execute(HttpClientRequest.get("https://api.example.com/users/1"))
  return yield* response.json
})

// Records if the cassette is missing, replays if it exists.
// In CI (CI=true) always replays — fails loudly on missing fixtures.
Effect.runPromise(program.pipe(Effect.provide(HttpRecorder.cassetteLayer("users/get-one"))))

// Force a refresh — always hits upstream and overwrites.
Effect.runPromise(program.pipe(Effect.provide(HttpRecorder.cassetteLayer("users/get-one", { mode: "record" })))))
```

--------------------------------

### Mintlify Tabbed Content Example

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/docs/ai-tools/cursor.mdx

Shows how to present alternative content using tabs, allowing users to select the information most relevant to their environment or operating system. Ideal for installation or configuration instructions across different platforms.

```markdown
<Tabs>
<Tab title="macOS">
  ```bash
  brew install node
  npm install -g package-name
  ```
</Tab>

<Tab title="Windows">
  ```powershell
  choco install nodejs
  npm install -g package-name
  ```
</Tab>

<Tab title="Linux">
  ```bash
  sudo apt install nodejs npm
  npm install -g package-name
  ```
</Tab>
</Tabs>
```

--------------------------------

### Mintlify Single Code Block Example

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/docs/ai-tools/cursor.mdx

Demonstrates how to display a single code snippet with language highlighting. This is useful for showing configuration files or short script examples.

```javascript
const apiConfig = {
  baseURL: "https://api.example.com",
  timeout: 5000,
  headers: {
    Authorization: `Bearer ${process.env.API_TOKEN}`,
  },
}
```

--------------------------------

### Mintlify Request/Response Example

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/docs/ai-tools/cursor.mdx

Illustrates how to document API requests and their corresponding responses. This helps users understand how to interact with an API and what to expect.

```bash
curl -X POST 'https://api.example.com/users' \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"name": "John Doe", "email": "john@example.com"}'
```

```json
{
  "id": "user_123",
  "name": "John Doe", 
  "email": "john@example.com",
  "created_at": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z"
}
```

--------------------------------

### Develop SolidStart Project with npm run dev

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/console/app/README.md

Starts the SolidStart development server. Dependencies must be installed first using 'npm install', 'pnpm install', or 'yarn'. The '-- --open' flag can be used to automatically open the application in a browser.

```bash
npm run dev

# or start the server and open the app in a new browser tab
npm run dev -- --open
```

--------------------------------

### Start development server for SolidStart project

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/enterprise/README.md

These commands are used to start the development server for a SolidStart project. After installing dependencies, `npm run dev` will launch the server. An additional option `--open` is provided to automatically open the application in a new browser tab upon server startup.

```bash
npm run dev

# or start the server and open the app in a new browser tab
npm run dev -- --open
```

--------------------------------

### Accordion Examples

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/docs/ai-tools/cursor.mdx

Demonstrates how to use Accordion and AccordionGroup components for collapsible content sections.

```APIDOC
## Accordions for collapsible content

Example of accordion groups:

<AccordionGroup>
<Accordion title="Troubleshooting connection issues">
  - **Firewall blocking**: Ensure ports 80 and 443 are open
  - **Proxy configuration**: Set HTTP_PROXY environment variable
  - **DNS resolution**: Try using 8.8.8.8 as DNS server
</Accordion>

<Accordion title="Advanced configuration">
  ```javascript
  const config = {
    performance: { cache: true, timeout: 30000 },
    security: { encryption: 'AES-256' }
  };
  ```
</Accordion>
</AccordionGroup>
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize or update AGENTS.md

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/tui.mdx

Starts a guided setup process for creating or updating the 'AGENTS.md' configuration file.

```bash
/init
```

--------------------------------

### Cards and Columns Examples

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/docs/ai-tools/cursor.mdx

Illustrates the use of Card and CardGroup components for emphasizing information, with options for column layouts.

```APIDOC
### Cards and columns for emphasizing information

Example of cards and card groups:

<Card title="Getting started guide" icon="rocket" href="/quickstart">
Complete walkthrough from installation to your first API call in under 10 minutes.
</Card>

<CardGroup cols={2}>
<Card title="Authentication" icon="key" href="/auth">
  Learn how to authenticate requests using API keys or JWT tokens.
</Card>

<Card title="Rate limiting" icon="clock" href="/rate-limits">
  Understand rate limits and best practices for high-volume usage.
</Card>
</CardGroup>
```

--------------------------------

### Create opencode client instance with server

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/sdk.mdx

This code shows how to initialize the opencode SDK, which starts both a local server and a client instance. It uses the `createOpencode` function from the SDK, providing a convenient way to get started with opencode programmatically.

```javascript
import { createOpencode } from "@opencode-ai/sdk"

const { client } = await createOpencode()
```

--------------------------------

### Provide feedback and context to OpenCode's plan

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/index.mdx

After OpenCode presents a plan, you can refine it by providing additional feedback or context. This example shows how to reference an external design (e.g., an image) to guide OpenCode's implementation of a new screen.

```txt
We'd like to design this new screen using a design I've used before.
[Image #1] Take a look at this image and use it as a reference.
```

--------------------------------

### Update Component Example

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/docs/ai-tools/cursor.mdx

Demonstrates the Update component for displaying changelogs, including version labels, release dates, new features, and bug fixes.

```jsx
<Update label="Version 2.1.0" description="Released March 15, 2024">
## New features
- Added bulk user import functionality
- Improved error messages with actionable suggestions

## Bug fixes

- Fixed pagination issue with large datasets
- Resolved authentication timeout problems
  </Update>
```

--------------------------------

### Install OpenCode using the official install script

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/index.mdx

This command provides the easiest and quickest way to install OpenCode on most Unix-like systems. It downloads and executes the official installation script, setting up OpenCode for immediate use.

```bash
curl -fsSL https://opencode.ai/install | bash
```

--------------------------------

### Setup Virtual Display for Headless Linux Environments

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/tr/troubleshooting.mdx

Install and configure a virtual display server for clipboard functionality in headless Linux environments without a physical display.

```bash
apt install -y xvfb
```

```bash
Xvfb :99 -screen 0 1024x768x24 > /dev/null 2>&1 &
export DISPLAY=:99.0
```

--------------------------------

### Tooltip Component Example

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/docs/ai-tools/cursor.mdx

Illustrates the usage of the Tooltip component to provide contextual information when hovering over text.

```jsx
<Tooltip tip="Application Programming Interface - protocols for building software">
API
</Tooltip>
```

--------------------------------

### Install opencode GitHub App via CLI

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/github/README.md

This command initiates the installation process for the opencode GitHub app directly from the terminal. It guides the user through setting up the GitHub app, creating the necessary workflow, and configuring secrets.

```bash
opencode github install
```

--------------------------------

### Start Development Server

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/opencode/AGENTS.md

Starts the live interactive TUI development server for `opencode`.

```bash
bun dev
```

--------------------------------

### Start Mintlify Development Server (bash)

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/docs/README.md

Starts the Mintlify development server to preview documentation changes locally. This command should be run at the root of the documentation project where 'docs.json' is located.

```bash
mint dev
```

--------------------------------

### Parameter Field Documentation

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/docs/ai-tools/cursor.mdx

Shows examples of documenting API parameters using ParamField for path, body, query, and header parameters.

```APIDOC
#### Parameter fields

Example of parameter documentation:

<ParamField path="user_id" type="string" required>
Unique identifier for the user. Must be a valid UUID v4 format.
</ParamField>

<ParamField body="email" type="string" required>
User's email address. Must be valid and unique within the system.
</ParamField>

<ParamField query="limit" type="integer" default="10">
Maximum number of results to return. Range: 1-100.
</ParamField>

<ParamField header="Authorization" type="string" required>
Bearer token for API authentication. Format: `Bearer YOUR_API_KEY`
</ParamField>
```

--------------------------------

### Install Mintlify CLI (npm)

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/docs/README.md

Installs the Mintlify Command Line Interface globally using npm. This CLI is used to preview documentation changes locally.

```bash
npm i -g mint
```

--------------------------------

### Create SolidStart Project with npm init

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/console/app/README.md

Commands to create a new SolidStart project using npm. This can be done in the current directory or a specified subdirectory. Ensure you have Node.js and npm installed.

```bash
# create a new project in the current directory
npm init solid@latest

# create a new project in my-app
npm init solid@latest my-app
```

--------------------------------

### Build SolidStart Project for Deployment

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/console/app/README.md

Builds the SolidStart project for deployment using presets. By default, it generates a Node.js application runnable with 'npm start'. Custom presets can be configured in 'app.config.js' and added to 'devDependencies'.

```bash
npm run build

npm start
```

--------------------------------

### Accordion Component Example

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/docs/ai-tools/cursor.mdx

Demonstrates the usage of Accordion components for organizing collapsible content. It includes a title for each accordion and content that can be text or code.

```jsx
<AccordionGroup>
<Accordion title="Troubleshooting connection issues">
  - **Firewall blocking**: Ensure ports 80 and 443 are open
  - **Proxy configuration**: Set HTTP_PROXY environment variable
  - **DNS resolution**: Try using 8.8.8.8 as DNS server
</Accordion>

<Accordion title="Advanced configuration">
  ```javascript
  const config = {
    performance: { cache: true, timeout: 30000 },
    security: { encryption: 'AES-256' }
  };
  ```
</Accordion>
</AccordionGroup>
```

--------------------------------

### api.plugins.install(spec, options?)

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/opencode/specs/tui-plugins.md

Install a plugin by specification. Downloads and installs a plugin with optional configuration.

```APIDOC
## api.plugins.install(spec, options?)

### Description
Install a plugin by specification with optional configuration options.

### Method
Function call

### Parameters
- **spec** (string|object) - Required - Plugin specification (package name or configuration object)
- **options** (object) - Optional - Installation options

### Usage
```typescript
api.plugins.install("plugin-package", { force: true });
```
```

--------------------------------

### Common Astro Project Commands

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/README.md

These are essential npm commands for managing your Astro project. They include installing dependencies, starting a development server, building for production, and previewing the build. Run these from the project's root directory.

```bash
npm install
npm run dev
npm run build
npm run preview
npm run astro ...
npm run astro -- --help
```

--------------------------------

### Card Component Example

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/docs/ai-tools/cursor.mdx

Illustrates the use of Card components for highlighting information, featuring titles, icons, and links. Card groups can be used to arrange multiple cards in columns.

```jsx
<Card title="Getting started guide" icon="rocket" href="/quickstart">
Complete walkthrough from installation to your first API call in under 10 minutes.
</Card>

<CardGroup cols={2}>
<Card title="Authentication" icon="key" href="/auth">
  Learn how to authenticate requests using API keys or JWT tokens.
</Card>

<Card title="Rate limiting" icon="clock" href="/rate-limits">
  Understand rate limits and best practices for high-volume usage.
</Card>
</CardGroup>
```

--------------------------------

### api.plugins.install(spec, options)

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/opencode/specs/tui-plugins.md

Installs a plugin using the provided spec and options. Runs install, manifest read, and config patch. Returns an object indicating success or failure with optional error details.

```APIDOC
## api.plugins.install(spec, options)

### Description
Installs a plugin using the provided spec and options.

### Method Signature
```
api.plugins.install(spec: string, options?: {global?: boolean}): {ok: boolean, message?: string, missing?: any, dir?: string, tui?: any}
```

### Parameters
- **spec** (string) - Required - The plugin spec to install.
- **options** (object) - Optional
  - **global** (boolean) - Optional - Whether to install globally.

### Returns
Object with one of two formats:
- Success: `{ ok: true, dir, tui }`
- Failure: `{ ok: false, message, missing? }`

### Behavior
- Runs install -> manifest read -> config patch using the same helper flow as CLI install.
- Does not load plugins into the current session. Call `api.plugins.add(spec)` to load after install.
```

--------------------------------

### Install OpenCode Desktop App on Windows with Scoop

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/README.md

Install the OpenCode desktop application on Windows using Scoop. First, add the 'extras' bucket, then install the application.

```bash
scoop bucket add extras; scoop install extras/opencode-desktop
```

--------------------------------

### Install Project Dependencies

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/app/README.md

Installs all necessary project dependencies using a package manager. This command prepares the environment for development.

```bash
npm install # or pnpm install or yarn install
```

--------------------------------

### Start OpenCode Web Interface Server

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/cli.mdx

Starts an HTTP server with a web interface and opens a browser. Set `OPENCODE_SERVER_PASSWORD` to enable HTTP basic auth.

```bash
opencode web
```

--------------------------------

### Install opencode for GitHub via CLI

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/pt-br/github.mdx

Run this command in a GitHub repository to automate the installation of the GitHub app and workflow configuration.

```bash
opencode github install
```

--------------------------------

### Use `provideTmpdirInstance` for custom instance setup

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/opencode/test/EFFECT_TEST_MIGRATION.md

Utilize `provideTmpdirInstance((dir) => effect, options)` for live tests needing custom instance setup or multiple instance scopes.

```ts
provideTmpdirInstance((dir) => effect, options)
```

--------------------------------

### Install Claude Code CLI Globally

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/docs/ai-tools/claude-code.mdx

Installs the Claude Code CLI tool globally using npm. This command requires Node.js and npm to be installed on your system. Once installed, the `claude` command will be available in your terminal.

```bash
npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize OpenCode for a project

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/index.mdx

Once OpenCode is configured, navigate to your project directory and start OpenCode. Then, run the `/init` command within OpenCode to analyze your project and generate an `AGENTS.md` file, which should be committed to your version control.

```bash
cd /path/to/project
```

```bash
opencode
```

```text
/init
```

--------------------------------

### Install OpenCode Plugin (CLI)

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/cli.mdx

Use these commands to install a plugin for OpenCode, either with the full command or its alias. Plugins extend OpenCode's functionality.

```bash
opencode plugin <module>
```

```bash
opencode plug <module>
```

--------------------------------

### Install OpenCode with cURL (YOLO)

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/README.md

Use this command for a quick installation of OpenCode via a direct cURL script. This method is often referred to as 'YOLO' for its simplicity.

```bash
curl -fsSL https://opencode.ai/install | bash
```

--------------------------------

### api.theme.install(jsonPath)

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/opencode/specs/tui-plugins.md

Installs a theme from a JSON file. Relative paths are resolved from the plugin root. Theme name is derived from the JSON filename. Installation behavior respects cross-process locking and plugin state.

```APIDOC
## api.theme.install(jsonPath)

### Description
Installs a theme JSON file.

### Method Signature
```
api.theme.install(jsonPath: string): void
```

### Parameters
- **jsonPath** (string) - Required - Path to the theme JSON file. Relative paths are resolved from the plugin root.

### Installation Behavior
- Theme name is derived from the JSON basename.
- Theme copy/write runs under cross-process lock key `tui-theme:<dest>`.
- First install writes only when the destination file is missing.
- If the theme name already exists, install is skipped unless plugin metadata state is `updated`.
- On `updated`, host skips rewrite when tracked `mtime`/`size` is unchanged.
- Local plugins persist installed themes under `.opencode/themes` near the plugin config source.
- Global plugins persist installed themes under the global `themes` directory.
- Invalid or unreadable theme files are ignored.
```

--------------------------------

### OpenCode Zen: Run Connect Command

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/providers.mdx

Initiate the provider connection process in the OpenCode TUI to set up OpenCode Zen.

```txt
/connect
```

--------------------------------

### OpenCode Zen: Run Models Command

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/providers.mdx

Display the list of recommended models after connecting to OpenCode Zen.

```txt
/models
```

--------------------------------

### Response Field Documentation

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/docs/ai-tools/cursor.mdx

Provides examples of documenting API response fields using ResponseField, including basic fields and expandable nested objects.

```APIDOC
#### Response fields

Example of response field documentation:

<ResponseField name="user_id" type="string" required>
Unique identifier assigned to the newly created user.
</ResponseField>

<ResponseField name="created_at" type="timestamp">
ISO 8601 formatted timestamp of when the user was created.
</ResponseField>

<ResponseField name="permissions" type="array">
List of permission strings assigned to this user.
</ResponseField>

#### Expandable nested fields

Example of nested field documentation:

<ResponseField name="user" type="object">
Complete user object with all associated data.

<Expandable title="User properties">
  <ResponseField name="profile" type="object">
  User profile information including personal details.
  
  <Expandable title="Profile details">
    <ResponseField name="first_name" type="string">
    User's first name as entered during registration.
    </ResponseField>
    
    <ResponseField name="avatar_url" type="string | null">
    URL to user's profile picture. Returns null if no avatar is set.
    </ResponseField>
  </Expandable>
  </ResponseField>
</Expandable>
</ResponseField>
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize opencode client and server

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/pt-br/sdk.mdx

This method starts both a local server and a client instance for immediate use.

```javascript
import { createOpencode } from "@opencode-ai/sdk"

const { client } = await createOpencode()
```

--------------------------------

### Install OpenCode using Mise version manager

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/index.mdx

If you are using Mise as your version manager, you can integrate OpenCode into your environment by adding the `anomalyco/opencode` plugin. This ensures OpenCode is managed alongside your other tools.

```bash
mise use -g github:anomalyco/opencode
```

--------------------------------

### Video Embedding

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/docs/ai-tools/cursor.mdx

Provides examples for embedding video content using the HTML video element for self-hosted videos and iframe elements for YouTube embeds.

```html
<video
controls
className="w-full aspect-video rounded-xl"
src="link-to-your-video.com">
</video>

<iframe
  className="w-full aspect-video rounded-xl"
  src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/4KzFe50RQkQ"
  title="YouTube video player"
  frameBorder="0"
  allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture"
  allowFullScreen
></iframe>
```

--------------------------------

### Setup Clipboard for Headless Linux Environments

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/troubleshooting.mdx

Install and configure Xvfb virtual framebuffer for clipboard functionality in headless Linux environments where no display server is available.

```bash
apt install -y xvfb
Xvfb :99 -screen 0 1024x768x24 > /dev/null 2>&1 &
export DISPLAY=:99.0
```

--------------------------------

### Instalar OpenCode Desktop via gerenciador de pacotes

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/README.br.md

Comandos para instalar a versão desktop do OpenCode usando Homebrew no macOS e Scoop no Windows.

```bash
brew install --cask opencode-desktop
```

```bash
scoop bucket add extras; scoop install extras/opencode-desktop
```

--------------------------------

### Create New Agent Interactively (Bash)

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/agents.mdx

Initiate an interactive command-line process to create a new agent, guiding through setup steps like saving location, description, prompt generation, and permission selection.

```bash
opencode agent create
```

--------------------------------

### Ask OpenCode questions about codebase files

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/index.mdx

After initializing OpenCode for your project, you can interact with it directly in the TUI to ask questions about your codebase. Use the `@` key to fuzzy search and reference specific files, making it easy to understand unfamiliar code sections.

```text
How is authentication handled in @packages/functions/src/api/index.ts
```

--------------------------------

### Add Test MCP Server Package

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/mcp-servers.mdx

Example configuration for adding the @modelcontextprotocol/server-everything test package as a local MCP server. This demonstrates how to use npx to run an npm package directly without prior installation.

```jsonc
{
  "$schema": "https://opencode.ai/config.json",
  "mcp": {
    "mcp_everything": {
      "type": "local",
      "command": ["npx", "-y", "@modelcontextprotocol/server-everything"]
    }
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Install OpenCode on Arch Linux

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/index.mdx

Arch Linux users have two options for installing OpenCode: from the stable repositories using `pacman` or obtaining the latest version from the Arch User Repository (AUR) via `paru`. Choose based on your preference for stability or cutting-edge features.

```bash
sudo pacman -S opencode
```

```bash
paru -S opencode-bin
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Atomic Chat provider in opencode.json

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/providers.mdx

Set up a local LLM provider using Atomic Chat with OpenAI-compatible API. Replace baseURL host/port to match your Atomic Chat setup and model IDs with those returned by GET /v1/models.

```json
{
  "$schema": "https://opencode.ai/config.json",
  "provider": {
    "atomic-chat": {
      "npm": "@ai-sdk/openai-compatible",
      "name": "Atomic Chat (local)",
      "options": {
        "baseURL": "http://127.0.0.1:1337/v1"
      },
      "models": {
        "<your-model-id>": {
          "name": "<your-model-name>"
        }
      }
    }
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Create a new SolidStart project using npm

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/enterprise/README.md

These commands demonstrate how to initialize a new SolidStart project. You can choose to create the project in the current directory or specify a new directory name for your application. This setup leverages the `solid@latest` initializer.

```bash
# create a new project in the current directory
npm init solid@latest

# create a new project in my-app
npm init solid@latest my-app
```

--------------------------------

### POST /project/init

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/specs/project.md

Initializes a new project in the OpenCode instance. Creates a new project with default configuration and returns the created Project object.

```APIDOC
## POST /project/init

### Description
Initialize a new project in the OpenCode instance.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/project/init

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **Project** (object) - The newly created project object

#### Response Example
{
  "id": "project-1",
  "name": "New Project",
  "createdAt": "2024-01-01T00:00:00Z"
}
```

--------------------------------

### Example: Access Windows files and run OpenCode in WSL

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/windows-wsl.mdx

This example demonstrates navigating to a project directory on a Windows drive (e.g., `C:`) from within a WSL terminal and then executing the `opencode` command. WSL automatically mounts Windows drives under the `/mnt/` directory, enabling seamless file access.

```bash
cd /mnt/c/Users/YourName/Documents/project
opencode
```

--------------------------------

### Directly request a code change from OpenCode

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/index.mdx

For straightforward modifications, you can ask OpenCode to make changes directly without a prior planning phase. Provide clear instructions, including file paths and examples, to guide OpenCode in implementing the desired logic.

```txt
We need to add authentication to the /settings route. Take a look at how this is
handled in the /notes route in @packages/functions/src/notes.ts and implement
the same logic in @packages/functions/src/settings.ts
```

--------------------------------

### Install OpenCode on Windows using package managers

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/index.mdx

OpenCode can be installed on Windows using Chocolatey, Scoop, or npm. While these methods are available, it is strongly recommended to use Windows Subsystem for Linux (WSL) for a superior experience with better performance and full compatibility.

```bash
choco install opencode
```

```bash
scoop install opencode
```

```bash
npm install -g opencode-ai
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Local MCP Server with Command and Environment

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/mcp-servers.mdx

Set up a local MCP server by specifying the command to start it and optional environment variables. The command is executed as an array of strings, and environment variables are passed as key-value pairs. This example uses npx to run a local MCP command.

```jsonc
{
  "$schema": "https://opencode.ai/config.json",
  "mcp": {
    "my-local-mcp-server": {
      "type": "local",
      "command": ["npx", "-y", "my-mcp-command"],
      "enabled": true,
      "environment": {
        "MY_ENV_VAR": "my_env_var_value"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Attach Terminal TUI to Running OpenCode Web Server

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/web.mdx

These commands illustrate how to first start an OpenCode web server on a specific port and then, in a separate terminal, attach a Text User Interface (TUI) to that running server. This setup allows users to simultaneously utilize both the web interface and a terminal, sharing the same sessions and state.

```bash
# Start the web server
opencode web --port 4096

# In another terminal, attach the TUI
opencode attach http://localhost:4096
```

--------------------------------

### Adding the MCP Everything Test Server

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/it/mcp-servers.mdx

Example configuration for the @modelcontextprotocol/server-everything package using npx.

```jsonc
{
  "$schema": "https://opencode.ai/config.json",
  "mcp": {
    "mcp_everything": {
      "type": "local",
      "command": ["npx", "-y", "@modelcontextprotocol/server-everything"],
    },
  },
}
```

--------------------------------

### GET /api/project/:projectID

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/specs/v2/api.html

Get project metadata.

```APIDOC
## GET /api/project/:projectID

### Description
Get project metadata.

### Method
GET

### Endpoint
/api/project/:projectID

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
- **projectID** (ProjectID) - Required - The ID of the project.
```

--------------------------------

### Add a custom LSP server

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/lsp.mdx

Configure a custom LSP server by specifying the command to start it and the file extensions it should handle.

```json
{
  "$schema": "https://opencode.ai/config.json",
  "lsp": {
    "custom-lsp": {
      "command": ["custom-lsp-server", "--stdio"],
      "extensions": [".custom"]
    }
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Customize OpenCode installation directory

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/README.md

Specify a custom installation directory for OpenCode using OPENCODE_INSTALL_DIR or XDG_BIN_DIR environment variables before running the install script.

```bash
OPENCODE_INSTALL_DIR=/usr/local/bin curl -fsSL https://opencode.ai/install | bash
XDG_BIN_DIR=$HOME/.local/bin curl -fsSL https://opencode.ai/install | bash
```

--------------------------------

### Install Clipboard Tools for X11 on Linux

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/tr/troubleshooting.mdx

Install clipboard utilities required for copy/paste functionality on X11-based Linux systems. Install at least one of these tools.

```bash
apt install -y xclip
```

```bash
apt install -y xsel
```

--------------------------------

### GET /api/auth/:accountID

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/specs/v2/api.html

Get one auth account.

```APIDOC
## GET /api/auth/:accountID

### Description
Get one auth account.

### Method
GET

### Endpoint
/api/auth/:accountID

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
- **accountID** (AccountID) - Required - The ID of the account to retrieve.
```

--------------------------------

### POST /project/:projectID/session/:sessionID/init

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/specs/project.md

Initializes a session, preparing it for use. Sets up necessary resources and configurations for the session.

```APIDOC
## POST /project/:projectID/session/:sessionID/init

### Description
Initialize a session for use.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/project/:projectID/session/:sessionID/init

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
- **projectID** (string) - Required - The unique identifier of the project
- **sessionID** (string) - Required - The unique identifier of the session

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **Session** (object) - The initialized session object

#### Response Example
{
  "id": "session-1",
  "projectID": "project-1",
  "status": "initialized"
}
```

--------------------------------

### Install OpenCode on macOS and Linux using Homebrew

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/index.mdx

Homebrew users on macOS and Linux can install OpenCode by tapping into the anomalyco repository. It is recommended to use this tap for the most current releases, as the main Homebrew formula may be updated less frequently.

```bash
brew install anomalyco/tap/opencode
```

--------------------------------

### GET /api/catalog/model/:providerID/:modelID

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/specs/v2/api.html

Get one catalog model.

```APIDOC
## GET /api/catalog/model/:providerID/:modelID

### Description
Get one catalog model.

### Method
GET

### Endpoint
/api/catalog/model/:providerID/:modelID

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
- **providerID** (ProviderID) - Required - The ID of the provider.
- **modelID** (ModelID) - Required - The ID of the model.
```

--------------------------------

### Install OpenCode Desktop App on macOS with Homebrew

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/README.md

Install the OpenCode desktop application on macOS using Homebrew Cask. This provides a convenient way to manage the desktop app installation.

```bash
brew install --cask opencode-desktop
```

--------------------------------

### Bootstrap or fire-and-forget startup code

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/opencode/specs/effect/facades.md

For initialization-only facade calls, use the existing runtime for that file and call the service through `Service.use(...)`. Do not reintroduce a dedicated runtime in the service.

```typescript
void BootstrapRuntime.runPromise(Vcs.Service.use((svc) => svc.init()))
```

--------------------------------

### api.plugins.list()

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/opencode/specs/tui-plugins.md

List all installed plugins. Returns information about all currently installed plugins.

```APIDOC
## api.plugins.list()

### Description
Retrieve a list of all installed plugins.

### Method
Function call

### Usage
```typescript
const plugins = api.plugins.list();
```

### Returns
- **plugins** (array) - Array of installed plugin information objects
```

--------------------------------

### Example tui.json Configuration File

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/tui.mdx

Illustrative `tui.json` file demonstrating various configuration options for the TUI, including theme, keybinds, scrolling, diff style, mouse, and attention settings.

```json
{
  "$schema": "https://opencode.ai/tui.json",
  "theme": "opencode",
  "leader_timeout": 2000,
  "keybinds": {
    "leader": "ctrl+x",
    "command_list": "ctrl+p"
  },
  "scroll_speed": 3,
  "scroll_acceleration": {
    "enabled": false
  },
  "diff_style": "auto",
  "mouse": true,
  "attention": {
    "enabled": true,
    "notifications": true,
    "sound": true,
    "volume": 0.4,
    "sound_pack": "opencode.default",
    "sounds": {
      "error": "./sounds/error.mp3"
    }
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Start OpenCode Web Interface

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/web.mdx

This command launches the OpenCode web server locally, binding to an available port on `127.0.0.1` and automatically opening the web interface in your default browser. It serves as the primary method to initiate the web application.

```bash
opencode web
```

--------------------------------

### Start a new session

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/tui.mdx

Clears the current conversation and starts a fresh session. Also accessible via '/clear'.

```bash
/new
```

--------------------------------

### Start Development Server in Tmux

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/opencode/AGENTS.md

Starts the `opencode` development server in a detached `tmux` session named `opencode-dev`.

```bash
tmux new-session -d -s opencode-dev 'bun dev'
```

--------------------------------

### Start OpenCode TUI with optional project

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/cli.mdx

Initiates the OpenCode Terminal User Interface, optionally specifying a project to load.

```bash
opencode [project]
```

--------------------------------

### OpenCode CLI Commands (Development vs. Production)

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/CONTRIBUTING.md

These commands illustrate how to use the OpenCode CLI during development with `bun dev` and in a production environment with the `opencode` executable, showing available commands and how to start different services.

```bash
# Development (from project root)
bun dev --help           # Show all available commands
bun dev serve            # Start headless API server
bun dev web              # Start server + open web interface
bun dev <directory>      # Start TUI in specific directory
```

```bash
# Production
opencode --help          # Show all available commands
opencode serve           # Start headless API server
opencode web             # Start server + open web interface
opencode <directory>     # Start TUI in specific directory
```

--------------------------------

### Install Clipboard Tools for Wayland on Linux

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/tr/troubleshooting.mdx

Install the clipboard utility required for copy/paste functionality on Wayland-based Linux systems.

```bash
apt install -y wl-clipboard
```

--------------------------------

### Install Helicone Session Plugin

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/providers.mdx

Install the opencode-helicone-session plugin globally to automatically log OpenCode conversations as sessions in Helicone.

```bash
npm install -g opencode-helicone-session
```

--------------------------------

### Create Astro Project with Starlight Template

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/README.md

This command initializes a new Astro project using the official Starlight template. Ensure you have Node.js and npm installed. This sets up the foundational structure for your documentation site.

```bash
npm create astro@latest -- --template starlight
```

--------------------------------

### Configure opencode client with custom options

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/sdk.mdx

This example demonstrates how to pass a configuration object to `createOpencode` to customize server behavior, such as hostname, port, and model settings. It allows overriding or adding to the default `opencode.json` configuration. The snippet also shows how to access the server URL and close the server instance.

```javascript
import { createOpencode } from "@opencode-ai/sdk"

const opencode = await createOpencode({
  hostname: "127.0.0.1",
  port: 4096,
  config: {
    model: "anthropic/claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022",
  },
})

console.log(`Server running at ${opencode.server.url}`)

opencode.server.close()
```

--------------------------------

### Example PR Titles

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/CONTRIBUTING.md

These examples demonstrate how to construct PR titles following conventional commit standards, with and without scope.

```text
docs: update contributing guidelines
```

```text
fix: resolve crash on startup
```

```text
feat: add dark mode support
```

```text
feat(app): add dark mode support
```

```text
fix(desktop): resolve crash on startup
```

```text
chore: bump dependency versions
```

--------------------------------

### GET /provider/auth

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/server.mdx

Gets the available authentication methods for each provider, mapped by provider ID.

```APIDOC
## GET /provider/auth

### Description
Get provider authentication methods

### Method
GET

### Endpoint
/provider/auth

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
(None)

#### Query Parameters
(None)

#### Request Body
(None)

### Request Example
{}

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **[providerID: string]** (array of ProviderAuthMethod) - Mapping of provider IDs to their authentication methods.

#### Response Example
{
  "provider123": []
}
```
